methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $129M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.0B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
GITAI, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $129M+ to date, is assessed against a universe of 9 private comparables and 1 public peer. The private peer set spans same-sector robotics companies at Series B through D stages, including Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C), Nimble Robotics ($1.0B, Series C), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), and Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applied a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical for Series C-to-next-stage advancement to the $129M capital raised proxy, yielding $219M. Peer-median analysis benchmarked GITAI against five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, yielding a median valuation of $1.6B with a range of $1.0B to $2.0B. The divergence between methods reflects the significant maturation gap between recent-stage capital deployed and peer valuations achieved at equivalent or slightly advanced stages.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $186M (floor) to $1.9B (ceiling), with a median cross-sectional estimate of $935M. Key uncertainties include GITAI's relative product maturity, customer concentration, and unit economics relative to the peer set, as well as market conditions affecting late-stage robotics financing since peer valuations were last marked.
